zh.Cegidfan, Montgomery, Montgomery, Wales,: 英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利
zh.Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾史福斯波利
zh.lifeSummary: David Edward Scriven是Mr. Scriven的兒子，於從1270年到1280年出生在英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利。他於大約1300年與Joyce Madoc ap Kadwgan Wenwin在英國史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1325年在英國史洛普爾史福斯波利。

zh-hans.Cegidfan, Montgomery, Montgomery, Wales,: 英国威尔斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利
zh-hans.Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England: 英国史洛普爾史福斯波利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: David Edward Scriven是Mr. Scriven的儿子，于从1270年到1280年出生在英国威尔斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利。他于大约1300年与Joyce Madoc ap Kadwgan Wenwin在英国史洛普爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1325年在英国史洛普爾史福斯波利。

nameMeanings.name: Scriven
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: occupational name for a professional scribe, copyist, or clerk, from Middle English scrivein ‘scribe’ (Old French escrivain, medieval Latin scribanus). Compare Scrivener .
habitational name from Scriven (Yorkshire), from Old English scræfen, screfen ‘hollow place, place with caves or pits’.

variants.name: Scrivener
meanings: English: occupational name for a professional scribe, copyist, or clerk, from Middle English scrivener ‘scribe’ (a derivative of scrivain). Compare Scriven .

variants.name: Skirvin
meanings: Scottish: possibly a variant of Scriven , with metathesis of -ri- to -ir-. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Scrivens
meanings: English: variant of Scriven , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: David Edward Scriven was born from 1270 to 1280, in Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom as the son of Mr. Scriven. He married Joyce Madoc ap Kadwgan Wenwin about 1300, in Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1325, in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England.

fr.Cegidfan, Montgomery, Montgomery, Wales,: Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: David Edward Scriven est né du 1270 au 1280, à Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, fils de Mr. Scriven. Il a épousé Joyce Madoc ap Kadwgan Wenwin environ 1300, à Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1325, à Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: David Edward Scriven wurde von 1270 bis 1280 in Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Mr. Scriven geboren. Er hat um 1300 in Shropshire, England Joyce Madoc ap Kadwgan Wenwin geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1325 in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: David Edward Scriven è nato il da 1270 a 1280 nel Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Galles, Regno Unito come figlio di Mr. Scriven. ja.lifeSummary: David Edward Scrivenはから1270年まで1280年にイギリスウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアモントゴメリーでMr. Scrivenの息子として生まれた。約1300年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアでJoyce Madoc ap Kadwgan Wenwinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1325年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアシュリュースブリーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: David Edward Scriven(은)는 1270년 부터 1280년 까지 영국 웨일스 몽고머이실르 몽고메리에서 Mr. Scriven의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1300년 영국 슈럽실르에서 Joyce Madoc ap Kadwgan Wenwin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1325년 영국 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: David Edward Scriven родился с 1270 по 1280 в местности монтгомири, монтгомиришир, Уэльс, Великобритания. Его отца звали Mr. Scriven. Он женился на женщине по имени Joyce Madoc ap Kadwgan Wenwin около 1300 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1325 г. в местности шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия.
